name: TransactionOperationContracts
description: Typed requests, results, and stable errors for immutable transactions and category-allocation history; generic initial evidence is a separate atomic effect.
schema_def: ledger.transaction.record input: accountId, canonical signedAmount, ZAR, transactionDate, optional postingDate, originalDescription, initialEvidence. Result: TransactionDetail plus evidenceId, explicit reconciliationState, category state, pool state, and instrument or cardholder known-or-unknown attribution. The same transaction commits with an initial unassigned pool event and independently unknown instrument and cardholder attribution events. Errors cover account, money, date, evidence identity, and lifecycle conflicts. If identical initial evidence is already actively linked to an identical canonical transaction, logical replay returns that original transaction outcome and creates no fact; a changed financial fact or evidence payload conflicts. transaction.get optionally returns transaction, lifecycle, category, pool, attribution, evidence links, reconciliation decisions, and relationships. category.assign and category.correct append one active allocation history. void and supersede append lifecycle history and retire incompatible relationships. Supersede replacement uses the record input and never silently transfers category, pool, attribution, evidence links, reconciliation decisions, or relationships. All mutations use common idempotency envelopes; initial evidence, transaction, and explicit dimension initialization commit together.
